title: Contributing to {distspec}
text: <dist_spec> carries distribution type head S3 class (e.g. c("gamma", "dist_spec")), per-type methods dispatch directly read parameters x$parameters (x$pmf nonparametric family). framework handles shared work: validating number samples, resolving distribution whose parameters priors. uncertain distribution given extra "uncertain_dist_spec" class, mean.uncertain_dist_spec()/sample_dist.uncertain_dist_spec() intercept method runs. per-type methods therefore ever see fixed, numeric parameters, written plain functions. new_dist_spec() runs validate_dist_spec() every object builds, malformed spec fails construction.

title: Contributing to {distspec}
text: Create R/<type>.R implement methods apply. natural_params() lower_bounds() always required. mean()/sd() need closed form, sample_dist() gives random draws, dist_cdf() distributions can discretised, to_natural() needed constructor accepts alternative parameterisation (e.g. mean/sd) must converted natural parameters.
code: # R/mydist.R -- methods for the "mydist" distribution. Each dispatches on the # "mydist" class of a `<dist_spec>` and reads fixed parameters from # `x$parameters`. Uncertainty and validation are handled by the framework, so # these methods are pure.  # Required: the names of the natural parameters, in order. #' @exportS3Method natural_params.mydist <- function(x) c("param1", "param2")  # Required: the lower bound of each parameter (include any `mean`/`sd` # alternative parameterisation the constructor accepts). #' @exportS3Method lower_bounds.mydist <- function(x) {   c(param1 = 0, param2 = 0) }  # Optional: the analytic mean. Omit if there is no closed form. #' @method mean mydist #' @export mean.mydist <- function(x, ...) {   x$parameters$param1 }  # Optional: the analytic standard deviation. Omit if there is no closed form. #' @method sd mydist #' @export sd.mydist <- function(x, ...) {   x$parameters$param2 }  # Optional: random draws, using the family's base-R generator. A composite # (multi-component) distribution is sampled per component automatically. #' @importFrom stats rmydist #' @exportS3Method sample_dist.mydist <- function(x, n, ...) {   rmydist(n, x$parameters$param1, x$parameters$param2) }  # Optional (alternative parameterisations): convert the parameters the # constructor accepts (e.g. `mean`/`sd`) to the natural parameters. Take the # parameter means from `x$parameters` and return a named list. #' @exportS3Method to_natural.mydist <- function(x) {   ux <- lapply(x$parameters, mean)   list(param1 = ux$param1, param2 = ux$param2) }  # Optional (discretisation): the CDF as a *function* whose arguments match the # natural parameters (e.g. a base-R `p*` function such as `pgamma`). Provide this # only if the distribution can be discretised; omit it otherwise (it then errors # informatively via `dist_cdf.default()`). #' @exportS3Method dist_cdf.mydist <- function(x) pmydist  # Optional (bespoke discretisation): if your distribution discretises in a # special way rather than through a CDF (as the point-mass `fixed` does), provide # a `discrete_pmf()` method instead of `dist_cdf()`: # # #' @exportS3Method # discrete_pmf.mydist <- function(x, max_value, ...) { #   ## return a numeric PMF vector computed from `x$parameters` # }

what: Define Probability Distributions with Certain or Uncertain Parameters
title: Define Probability Distributions with Certain or Uncertain Parameters
text: distspec represents probability distribution single object, <dist_spec>, whose parameters can either fixed uncertain. grew EpiNow2 aimed delay distributions common infectious disease modelling, generation times, incubation periods reporting delays, staying independent particular model. distspec can: define distribution named constructor (Gamma(), LogNormal(), Normal(), Exponential(), Weibull(), Beta(), Fixed() NonParametric()), natural parameters mean standard deviation; give parameter uncertain prior (example Gamma(shape = Normal(2, 0.5), rate = 1)), leave nonparametric mass function uncertain via Dirichlet() prior; discretise continuous distribution probability mass function (discretise()), convolve distributions (+, collapse()), draw samples (sample_dist()), query means, standard deviations, bounds PMFs.

title: Dirichlet prior for a nonparametric distribution — Dirichlet
Dirichlet prior for a nonparametric distribution — Dirichlet
text: alpha positive numeric vector concentration parameters. prior Either numeric PMF vector (zero-indexed, .e. first entry represents probability mass zero) dist_spec object. dist_spec object provided discretised PMF extracted. numeric, normalised sum one internally. concentration positive scalar controlling tightly Dirichlet prior concentrates around supplied PMF. Dirichlet alpha vector computed alpha_i = concentration * p_i p_i prior PMF. Guidance values: concentration = 1: weak prior, alpha equals PMF value (near-uniform roughly equal PMF entries) concentration = 5-20: moderate flexibility around reference shape concentration = 50+: strong anchoring reference PMF ... used.

title: Define bounds of a <dist_spec> — bound_dist
text: Set bounds constrain distribution discretised: max truncates support value, cdf_cutoff trims tail keeping distribution cdf_cutoff quantile. Either bound drops mass beyond renormalises remaining PMF sum one.

title: Define bounds of a <dist_spec> — bound_dist
Define bounds of a <dist_spec> — bound_dist
text: x <dist_spec>. max Numeric, maximum value distribution. distribution truncated value. Default: Inf, .e. maximum. cdf_cutoff Numeric (0, 1]; cumulative probability distribution kept, .e. truncated cdf_cutoff quantile. example cdf_cutoff = 0.999 keeps distribution 99.9th percentile. Default: 1, .e. keep full distribution. value 0.5 rejected, almost certainly tail probability drop rather CDF level keep (use 1 - x instead).

what: Delta-method standard deviations of the natural parameters — delta_method_sd
title: Delta-method standard deviations of the natural parameters — delta_method_sd
text: First-order (delta-method) standard deviations distribution's natural parameters, propagated uncertain unnatural parameters. uncertain unnatural parameters treated independent normals standard deviations sds, natural parameter propagated standard deviation sqrt(sum_i J[j, ]^2 * sds[]^2), Jacobian J[j, ] = d(natural_j) / d(param_i) estimated central finite differences. Estimating Jacobian numerically lets work uniformly every distribution type, including Weibull, whose to_natural() solves shape numerically.

title: Delta-method standard deviations of the natural parameters — delta_method_sd
Delta-method standard deviations of the natural parameters — delta_method_sd
text: x single <dist_spec> whose parameters unnatural parameters evaluated means. sds Numeric; standard deviation unnatural parameter, names(x$parameters) order (0 fixed parameter). natural natural-parameter list evaluated means. h_rel Numeric; relative step central finite difference. parameter step h_rel * max(abs(mean_i), 1), .e. relative parameter value absolute floor near zero. default 1e-4 keeps truncation error (order h^2) floating-point cancellation error (order eps / h) small smooth to_natural() maps. numerical-differentiation constant exposed testing, deliberately kept user-facing constructors.

code: # A fixed gamma distribution with mean 5 and sd 1, discretised to a PMF. dist1 <- Gamma(mean = 5, sd = 1, max = 20) get_pmf(discretise(dist1)) #>  [1] 7.460101e-12 5.497386e-06 2.558827e-03 6.103148e-02 2.678533e-01 #>  [6] 3.692708e-01 2.174276e-01 6.734093e-02 1.271811e-02 1.628790e-03 #> [11] 1.528296e-04 1.112675e-05 6.564278e-07 3.244733e-08 1.379517e-09 #> [16] 5.150880e-11 1.720402e-12 4.851675e-14 3.330669e-15  # An uncertain lognormal distribution cannot be discretised, so with # `strict = FALSE` it is returned unchanged. dist2 <- LogNormal(   meanlog = Normal(3, 0.5),   sdlog = Normal(2, 0.5),   max = 20 ) discretise(dist2, strict = FALSE) #> - lognormal distribution (max: 20): #>   meanlog: #>     - normal distribution: #>       mean: #>         3 #>       sd: #>         0.5 #>   sdlog: #>     - normal distribution: #>       mean: #>         2 #>       sd: #>         0.5  # A fractional fixed value splits its mass across the two adjacent integers. get_pmf(discretise(Fixed(2.25))) #> [1] 0.00 0.00 0.75 0.25

title: distspec 0.1.0
text: Distribution constructors now validate structure object build (class, parameters, max/cdf_cutoff attributes) raise informative error malformed. Added Beta() distribution (shape1/shape2, mean/sd). Added Exponential() Weibull() distributions. Added Dirichlet() support uncertain nonparametric distributions specified via Dirichlet prior (NonParametric(pmf = Dirichlet(...))). Added sample_dist() draw random samples distribution fixed parameters. composite distribution sampled per component, returning n k matrix (rowSums() gives samples combined distribution). Distributions uncertain (prior) parameters sampled raise error. Added has_uncertainty(), predicate whether <dist_spec> (component composite) carries prior, dependent packages internal code can test uncertainty one place. Uncertainty distribution specified non-natural parameters (e.g. Gamma(mean = Normal(4, 0.5), sd = 1)) now propagated natural parameters first-order delta-method approximation. replaces ad-hoc rule understated natural-parameter standard deviations several times . discretise() gains remove_trailing_zeros argument (default TRUE). Exported lower-level helpers sd(), ndist(), natural_params() lower_bounds() dependent packages can reuse . natural_params() lower_bounds() accept distribution type given name (e.g. natural_params("gamma")), well <dist_spec>, dependent packages can query type metadata without constructing instance.

title: distspec 0.1.0
text: package renamed dist.spec distspec. distribution’s type now carried S3 class <dist_spec> (e.g. c("gamma", "dist_spec")), per-type behaviour dispatches directly distribution’s methods live one place. internal distribution dispatch class new_dist() removed. internal helpers natural_params() lower_bounds() now take <dist_spec> rather distribution-name string. get_parameters() now S3 generic. cdf_cutoff argument (distribution constructors bound_dist()) cumulative probability keep : cdf_cutoff = 0.999 truncates 99.9th percentile, default 1 keeps full distribution. value 0.5 rejected, almost certainly tail probability drop (use 1 - x).

title: distspec 0.1.0
text: Exp() deprecated favour Exponential().

title: distspec 0.1.0
text: NonParametric() Dirichlet(prior = ) now reject numeric PMF weight vector contains negative non-finite values, zero, informative error, instead silently producing invalid distribution. Un-normalised non-negative weights still accepted normalised. distribution parameter given certain distribution (standard deviation 0, e.g. Normal(x, 0), collapses Fixed(x)) now resolved point value construction, behaves exactly like passing number. Previously parameter left distribution marked uncertain, mean() sd() returned NA otherwise fully-fixed distribution (e.g. Gamma(shape = Normal(3, 0), rate = 2)). sd() nonparametric distribution now returns standard deviation rather variance (missing square root). also affects sd() discretised distribution, since discretise() produces nonparametric distribution. collapse() now correctly convolves runs three consecutive nonparametric distributions, runs begin first component, rather erroring convolving wrong component. Convolution collapse() now uses numerically stable implementation. bound_dist() now truncates fixed nonparametric PMF max PMF longer max + 1, renormalising result, leaves untouched max reaches beyond support. Previously condition inverted, bound never applied requested produced -NA PMF max exceeded support. Comparing two distributions == (!=) longer errors parameter numeric vector length greater one; parameters now compared whole vectors. fix_parameters() discretise() now forward strategy remove_trailing_zeros components composite distribution, arguments longer silently ignored composites. Fixed() distributions may now take value 0; lower bound value parameter corrected accordingly, value bound now rejected informative error instead silently producing invalid probability mass function. uncertain (Dirichlet-backed) nonparametric distribution now treated consistently uncertain, storing Dirichlet prior place concrete PMF just uncertain parametric distribution stores dist_spec parameter. PMF resolved fix_parameters(): get_pmf() errors distribution, mean() returns NA (prior mean ignore_uncertainty = TRUE), prints prior nested like uncertain distribution. Applying max cdf_cutoff uncertain (Dirichlet-backed) nonparametric distribution now raises informative error, since support fixed Dirichlet prior bound otherwise silently ignored. plot() gives actionable error asked plot distribution finite range (finite max cdf_cutoff), pointing bound_dist(), rather cryptic message silently chosen default range. mean() sd() now emit informative message return NA distribution uncertain parameters, pointing mean(x, ignore_uncertainty = TRUE) fix_parameters(). Improved error messages get_element() get_parameters(): --range id now reports offending value valid range, nonparametric error longer implies Weibull, Beta Exponential distributions lack parameters.

title: distspec 0.1.0
text: distribution now reference page (Gamma(), LogNormal(), …) rather single combined page, shows parameters. reference index covers full exported API, discretise() help page documents discretisation works, including fixed point-mass special case. Documentation improvements: getting-started vignette now shows end--end get_pmf(collapse(discretise(d1 + d2))) pipeline combining two delays single PMF, stale EpiNow2 Stan references removed roxygen, bound_dist(), discretise(), fix_parameters() sd() help pages clearer descriptions runnable examples.

title: distspec 0.1.0
text: Discretisation now uses primarycensored package compute double censored probability mass functions. natural_params() lower_bounds() now S3 generics, distribution’s behaviour defined alongside type (R/ file) rather scattered switch()/statements. Gamma(), Normal(), LogNormal(), Exp(), Weibull(), Beta(), Fixed(), Dirichlet() prior nonparametric distribution now define per-type behaviour (parameter metadata, mean()/sd()/max() applicable) way. internal per-distribution switch() statements collapsed direct S3 dispatch; attempting discretise distribution CDF now reports directly. Internal idiomatic cleanups: switched uncertain-parameter checks existing has_uncertainty() predicate (removing near-duplicate helper), threaded pre-computed parameter means to_natural() avoid redundant lapply(x$parameters, mean) calls every method, vectorised attribute-copy loop discretise(), used %||% null-default attribute guards, extracted repeated get_parameters() calls sum(convolutions) local variables. Reduced dependencies: dropped data.table, checkmate purrr, moved ggplot2 Suggests. plot() now prompts install ggplot2 missing, longer hard dependency package.
